name: Río Abión Gorge and La Pedriza Trail
name_original: Ruta de la Pedriza y Hoz del río Abión – running circular desde El Burgo de Osma
description: This moderate 6.1-mile jogging route from El Burgo de Osma combines historical views with the natural beauty of the Hoz del río Abión gorge.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma traverse a landscape characterized by its historical towns, river valleys, and surrounding countryside. The region offers a mix of flat paths along waterways and routes with gentle elevation changes through rural areas. These running routes provide opportunities to explore the natural and cultural features of the area. Best jogging routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma The most popular jogging route is Río Abión Gorge and La Pedriza Trail, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of river scenery and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from San Esteban de Gormaz, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This route offers a relatively flat terrain, ideal for a consistent pace. Local runners also love the El Burgo de Osma – View of Osma Castle loop from El Burgo de Osma, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through the local countryside with views of Osma Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma is defined by river paths, open fields, and historical town surroundings. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more challenging runs. The routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes in the Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma area, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging runs. Komoot users have recorded 17 tours in total, offering a variety of options for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Yes, for beginners or those looking for an easier pace, there is at least one easy route available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 4 km long with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in this region?
A: The running routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma offer a diverse landscape. You'll find a mix of flat paths along river valleys, such as the Río Abión, and routes with gentle elevation changes through the surrounding countryside and historical town areas. Expect a blend of natural trails and paths near local landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Many of the routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo de Osma – View of Osma Castle loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option offering scenic views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially those in natural, rural settings. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly near livestock or in more populated areas, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas. The river paths and countryside loops are usually suitable for running with dogs.

Q: What are some scenic jogging routes with good viewpoints?
A: For scenic views, consider routes that traverse the local countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo de Osma – View of Osma Castle loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent vistas of the historic Osma Castle. Routes along the Río Abión also provide picturesque river scenery.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: Routes with relatively flat terrain and shorter distances are ideal for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, shorter option that could be suitable for families looking for a gentle run or walk together.

Q: What is the average length and duration of running routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The running routes vary in length, but many fall within the 5 to 13 km range. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064181923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Abión Gorge and La Pedriza Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 9.9 km and takes around 1 hour 12 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from San Esteban de Gormaz&lt;/a&gt; is 8 km and can be completed in about 50 minutes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The running routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma are well-regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.3 stars. Runners often appreciate the mix of historical sights, river scenery, and tranquil countryside paths, providing a varied and enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running trails worth exploring?
A: While popular routes are well-known, exploring some of the moderate trails not highlighted in the introduction can reveal hidden gems. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya de Quintanilla – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from San Esteban de Gormaz&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a slightly longer, engaging run through the local landscape that might be less frequented.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in this region, with mild temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making year-round running possible, though some paths might be damp after rain.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or parking near the running routes?
A: As Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma is a historic town, you'll find various amenities within or close to the town center, including cafes and restaurants. Many routes start from or pass through areas where parking is available, especially near the towns of El Burgo de Osma and San Esteban de Gormaz, making access convenient.
tips:
The Hermitage of Las Magdalenas is located in El Burgo de Osma, Soria. This hermitage, built in the 13th century, stands out for its Gothic architecture and its majestic façade. Inside, it houses an impressive collection of paintings and sculptures from various periods, which show the devotion to Saint Mary Magdalene.

Located on top of a hill, the hermitage offers spectacular panoramic views of the city and its surroundings, making it an attractive place for both art lovers and nature lovers.
At this point we find ourselves in front of the Burgo East Watchtower, a 10th century construction in a strategic enclave dominating a large territory.

Osma Castle, located in El Burgo de Osma, Soria, is one of the oldest Christian fortresses in Spain. From the castle, you can enjoy stunning views of the confluence of the Abión and Ucero rivers, as well as the steep hill where the ruins of the ancient Celtiberian and Roman city of Uxama Argaela are located.

The castle has been recently restored, allowing visitors to explore its various structures and historical elements, such as the keep, the loopholes and the walls. In addition, there are informative signs and walkways that facilitate mobility and understanding of the site.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: The Route of the Pedriza and Hoz of the river Abión is approximately 9.87 kilometers long and typically takes about 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete as a jogging route. This duration can vary based on your pace and any stops you make to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate run, requiring good fitness. While generally accessible, some sections may require sure-footedness due to varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter mostly accessible paths, including unpaved and paved tracks, gravel paths, and some sections on streets and national roads. Specifically, it features about 4.76 km of path, 3.37 km of trail, and 1.66 km of street. The Hoz del río Abión section involves running between pines on increasingly sandy terrain before leveling out.

faqs.question: Is this running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is a favorite for runners and walkers with dogs, offering a more natural and quieter experience compared to other local paths. Please ensure your dog is kept under control, and check for any local leash regulations.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of historical and natural sights. You'll pass the 13th-century Gothic Ermita de las Magdalenas and El Burgo de Osma Viewpoint, offering expansive views. You'll also get panoramic views of the restored View of Osma Castle and pass by the Atalaya Este del Burgo. The Hoz del río Abión gorge itself is a beautiful natural highlight with pine forests.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from El Burgo de Osma. You can typically find parking within the town itself, which serves as the convenient starting point for this trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the area with vibrant foliage or blooming flora. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Route of the Pedriza and Hoz of the river Abión.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Ruta del Sureste, El Destierro, Camino del Cid (Senderista), and GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT.

faqs.question: Are there any specific navigation tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Since the trail does not have official signage, it is highly recommended to use navigation tools, such as a GPS device or a mapping app like komoot, to ensure you stay on track and fully enjoy the experience.

faqs.question: What makes this route unique compared to other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its unique blend of historical exploration and natural immersion directly accessible from El Burgo de Osma. It offers a more natural and quieter experience than the more urbanized Ucero river walk, making it ideal for those seeking both cultural sights and serene natural beauty.

waypoints.name: Ermita de las Magdalenas and El Burgo de Osma Viewpoint
